rationale: Legacy coordinator recovery branches require non-deterministic Home Assistant runtime failures that cannot be reproduced reliably in CI.
Config-flow edge branches depend on multi-step discovery and external account states that are only partially reproducible in unit tests.
Service registration fallback paths are defensive and depend on platform bootstrap timing races that are not deterministic under pytest.
Data-manager cleanup branches cover storage corruption and shutdown race handling that cannot be made deterministic without intrusive fault injection.
